news_items.headline: Bluebird Banter 2026 MLB Draft Preview Part 2
news_items.summary: Yesterday, I went over the details of the draft and the Jays’ potential strategy. Today, a quick review of the farm, the shape of the draft class, and some names. The Farm Baseball America refreshed their farm system rankings the day before yesterday, in anticipation of the draft. They currently have the Jays 18th. Fangraphs […]

news_items.headline: Blue Jays' Cease loses no-hit bid vs. Giants in 9t...
news_items.summary: The Blue Jays' Dylan Cease took a no-hitter into the ninth inning Wednesday before the Giants' Heliot Ramos broke it up on a line-drive single to center field.

news_items.headline: Boston's Contreras exits after fouling ball off fo...
news_items.summary: The ups and downs of Willson Contreras' season continued Wednesday when he had to leave the Red Sox's win after fouling a ball off his left foot.

news_items.headline: Angels' Trout comes off IL ahead of All-Star Game
news_items.summary: Mike Trout came off the injured list Wednesday and homered in his return, helping lift the Los Angeles Angels to a 13-1 victory over the Texas Rangers.

news_items.headline: Brewers find new injury to Woodruff's shoulder
news_items.summary: The Brewers said an MRI exam revealed a new injury to Brandon Woodruff's anterior capsule and are seeking a second opinion after initially diagnosing him with inflammation.

news_items.headline: Mattingly interested in managing Phillies full tim...
news_items.summary: With the Phillies firmly in playoff contention heading into the All-Star break, interim manager Don Mattingly said he would be interested in leading the club full time.

news_items.headline: All-Star Dingler hit by foul tip, exits Tigers til...
news_items.summary: Tigers All-Star catcher Dillon Dingler left Wednesday's game with a injured right hand. The Athletics' Nick Kurtz left in the second inning due to illness.

news_items.headline: White Sox call No. 1 draft pick a three-man race
news_items.summary: The White Sox have homed in on three candidates for the No. 1 pick in the MLB amateur draft: UCLA shortstop Roch Cholowsky, Georgia Tech catcher Vahn Lackey and Texas high school shortstop Grady Emerson.
